comment: Quality Gates catalog — structure dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/structure/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/structure/.

asserts: find docs scripts -type f | grep -E 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | grep -v CHANGELOG returns empty stdout

rows.owner_hint: any new doc/script that hardcodes a version in its filename outside CHANGELOG.md and *-phases/

asserts: pkg-mgr command (brew install | cargo binstall | curl|sh | powershell-irm) appears BEFORE any 'git clone https://' or 'cargo build --release' source-compile snippet in docs/index.md

rows.owner_hint: docs/index.md hero section — install path order

asserts: pkg-mgr command (brew install | cargo binstall | curl|sh | powershell-irm) appears BEFORE any 'git clone https://' or 'cargo build --release' source-compile snippet in README.md

rows.owner_hint: README.md hero section — install path order

asserts: every docs/benchmarks/*.md filename appears in mkdocs.yml

rows.owner_hint: mkdocs.yml `nav:` block — benchmarks subsection

asserts: find .planning/milestones -maxdepth 2 ( -name '*ROADMAP*' -o -name '*REQUIREMENTS*' ) | grep -v phases | grep -v archive returns empty stdout

rows.owner_hint: any *ROADMAP*.md / *REQUIREMENTS*.md at .planning/milestones/ top-level (must be inside *-phases/)

asserts: bash quality/gates/docs-build/mkdocs-strict.sh exits 0 (mkdocs --strict + orphan-doc check)

rows.owner_hint: mkdocs.yml `nav:` or `not_in_nav:` or `redirect_maps:`

asserts: top-level .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md contains exactly one '## v0.12.0 Requirements' section; the file does NOT contain '## v0.11.0' / '## v0.10.0' / '## v0.9.0' / '## v0.8.0' as top-level H2 sections (these belong inside .planning/milestones/v0.X.0-phases/REQUIREMENTS.md)

top-level .planning/ROADMAP.md contains exactly one '## v0.12.0 Quality Gates (PLANNING)' section; the file does NOT contain '## v0.11.0 Polish & Reproducibility (PLANNING)' / '## v0.10.0 Docs & Narrative Shine (PLANNING)' / '## v0.9.0 Architecture Pivot' as top-level H2 sections (these belong inside .planning/milestones/v0.X.0-phases/ROADMAP.md)
the historical milestones index paragraph (one paragraph at the top with relative links to .planning/milestones/v0.X.0-phases/{REQUIREMENTS,ROADMAP}.md) is permitted in both files

rows.owner_hint: if RED: move historical sections from .planning/{REQUIREMENTS,ROADMAP}.md to .planning/milestones/v0.X.0-phases/{REQUIREMENTS,ROADMAP}.md per POLISH-STRUCT (P57 Wave D)

asserts: P57 stub: row exists with verifier.script pointing at quality/gates/docs-build/badges-resolve.py (P60 ships the verifier per BADGE-01 traceability); the row anchors the contract from P57 onward; status: NOT-VERIFIED is acceptable until P60 ships the verifier.

rows.owner_hint: P60 docs-build dimension ships the verifier; for P57 the row's existence is the contract

asserts: bash quality/gates/structure/banned-words.sh exits 0 (no banned-word violations in docs/)

rows.owner_hint: any docs/ file using banned terms (FUSE, fusermount, kernel, partial-clone, etc. — see docs/.banned-words.toml for the full matrix)

asserts: no committed file on the outgoing ref-range matches the credential-prefix regex set: ATATT3[A-Za-z0-9_+/=-]{20,} (Atlassian); Bearer ATATT3 ; ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,} (GitHub classic PAT); github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{20,} (GitHub fine-grained PAT); AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35} (Google API key, added after a secret-scanning alert that the owner triaged as a false positive but which exposed a real pattern-coverage gap); AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} (AWS access key id); xox[baprs]-[0-9A-Za-z-]{10,} (Slack token); sk-(proj-)?[A-Za-z0-9]{20,} (OpenAI API key -- alnum-run form, tightened from sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,} to avoid kebab-case doc-slug collisions); [-]----BEGIN( RSA| EC| OPENSSH)? PRIVATE KEY----- (PEM private-key header; leading [-] char class avoids grep parsing the pattern as an option) -- the four AWS/Slack/OpenAI/PEM patterns added 2026-07-04 (D-CONV-4) as the zero-dep local layer beside the gitleaks CI backstop

the verifier respects the same EXCLUDE_DIRS as the existing hook (.git, target, node_modules, .githooks, quality/gates/structure -- the last is a load-bearing self-exclusion so the gate doesn't flag its own pattern literals)
the verifier exits 1 on any hit so pre-push BLOCKS the push (matches existing hook semantics; non-negotiable per CLAUDE.md threat model)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a real token shape was committed -- ROTATE THE TOKEN at the issuing platform BEFORE doing anything else (Atlassian: https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens; GitHub: https://github.com/settings/tokens; Google: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials)

asserts: find .planning -maxdepth 1 -type f \( -name '*MILESTONE-AUDIT*.md' -o -name 'SESSION-END-STATE*' \) | grep -v archive returns empty stdout

rows.owner_hint: any new milestone-audit or session-end-state document at .planning/ top level -- relocate under .planning/milestones/audits/ or .planning/archive/

asserts: For each docs/*.md whose byte size <500 AND whose top-level slug appears as a key in mkdocs.yml plugins.redirects.redirect_maps, the redirect target resolves to a file present under docs/. Stubs without a redirect entry AND without a nav: entry are flagged.

rows.owner_hint: any new top-level docs/<slug>.md stub <500 bytes -- add to mkdocs.yml redirect_maps or remove

asserts: File quality/reports/audits/repo-org-gaps.md exists

File contains a top-section with audit table mapping every numbered gap from .planning/research/v0.11.1/repo-organization-gaps.md 'Top 10 cleanup recommendations' to a closure path (closed-by-catalog-row | closed-by-existing-gate | waived)

rows.owner_hint: Wave 2 produces the audit; future repo-org audits land at the same path

asserts: File quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json exists and json.loads parses without error
Top-level keys schema_version, summary, rows are all present
schema_version equals '1.0'

rows.owner_hint: if RED: quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json missing or unparseable -- restore from P64 Wave 1 commit OR recover via /reposix-quality-backfill

asserts: summary block has all 9 required keys: claims_total, claims_bound, claims_missing_test, claims_retire_proposed, claims_retired, alignment_ratio, floor, trend_30d, last_walked
alignment_ratio equals claims_bound / max(1, claims_total - claims_retired) within 0.001 epsilon (when claims_total == 0, ratio is 1.0 by definition)
floor is a number in [0.0, 1.0]

rows.owner_hint: if RED: summary block hand-edited or runner mutation diverged from row counts -- run /reposix-quality-refresh quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json (or fix manually)

sources: .planning/research/v0.12.0-docs-alignment-design/02-architecture.md:Floor only ratchets up via deliberate human commit

asserts: git log -p --follow -- quality/catalogs/doc-alignment.json shows the floor field never decreased between commits (monotone non-decreasing)
floor regressions are an audit signal of an accidental ratchet-down; the commit SHA is named in the artifact for owner triage

rows.owner_hint: if RED: an earlier commit ratcheted the floor DOWN (audit signal) -- ratchet floor only via deliberate human commit, never auto

asserts: target/release/reposix-quality (or target/debug/reposix-quality fallback) exists and is executable
reposix-quality walk exits 0 when every row's source_hash + test_body_hash match the on-disk content
reposix-quality walk exits non-zero on any blocking row state (STALE_DOCS_DRIFT, MISSING_TEST, STALE_TEST_GONE, TEST_MISALIGNED, RETIRE_PROPOSED) AND prints the slash-command hint to stderr verbatim
the walker NEVER refreshes stored hashes; only `bind` does, and only after grader returns GREEN (asserted by crates/reposix-quality/tests/walk.rs)
on the empty-state catalog (P64 Wave 1; rows == []), the walker exits 0

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs-alignment hash drift detected -- run /reposix-quality-refresh <doc> from a fresh top-level Claude session (cannot run from inside /gsd-execute-phase). Stderr names the affected doc.

asserts: release-plz.toml exists at repo root
release-plz.toml contains a [workspace] table
release-plz.toml contains 'git_release_enable = false' (per-package GitHub release creation disabled so the canonical v* release stays releases/latest)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: someone deleted release-plz.toml or flipped git_release_enable. The fix is preventive — without it, release-plz creates one zero-asset GH release per workspace package on every push to main, stealing releases/latest from the canonical v* release. See PR #34 (commit f1d89e5) for the original rationale.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If per-package GitHub releases were re-enabled (release-plz.toml deleted, or git_release_enable flipped away from false), the verifier's grep for the exact file/table/key would come back empty or find the wrong value, exiting non-zero and flipping the row to FAIL -- directly falsifying the claim that per-package releases stay disabled and releases/latest stays pointed at the canonical v* tag.

asserts: every tracked file is within its extension's progressive-disclosure budget (CLAUDE.md=40k, .claude/skills/=10k, *.md=20k, *.rs=20k, *.py=15k, *.sh=10k); auto-generated + crates source excluded
Cargo.lock, quality/catalogs/*.json, quality/reports/, crates/.../tests/fixtures/, CHANGELOG.md, and crates/.../*.rs are excluded from scope
verifier exit 1 on any violation by default; --warn-only flag flips exit to 0 (transitional cleanup window)

rows.owner_hint: if violations: split the file per the hint emitted by the verifier (CLAUDE.md=40k, .claude/skills/=10k, *.md=20k, *.rs=20k, *.py=15k, *.sh=10k). Auto-generated/lockfile/catalog/fixture/CHANGELOG paths are excluded; *.rs in crates/ is also excluded pending source-code-budget cleanup in a future milestone.

waiver.reason: Re-instates the original 'warn-now, block-later' contract from commit 8ff9a66 that introduced this gate. Verifier args now carry --warn-only so the verifier exits 0 with a structured warning list; the catalog row stays visible (status FAIL surfaces the count) but pre-push does not block. Current violations: 10 files — 6 are research bundles in .planning/research/v0.13.0-real-backend-frictions/ (REMEDIATION-PLAN, vision-audit, COMPLETENESS-CHECK, PATTERNS, STRATEGIC-REFRAME, phase-audit-p88) where the natural-document-shape doesn't split well into <20k chunks; 3 are research bundles in .planning/research/v0.13.2-cross-link-fidelity/ (02-architecture, 03-schemas, 06-decisions-log); 1 is AGENTS.md, a SYMLINK to CLAUDE.md (which has its own 40k budget) — verifier should exclude symlinks but doesn't yet (separate fix). Target work to drain: split the natural-chapter research bundles into per-chapter child docs OR adopt mkdocs-material's 'composable docs' pattern; teach verifier to follow symlinks. Tracked as a +2 reservation candidate for the next milestone after v0.13.0/v0.13.2 ships.
waiver.dimension_owner: structure dimension owner (currently maintained by orchestrator)

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a tracked file blew past its extension's progressive-disclosure budget, the verifier's per-extension byte-count scan would report the specific path + size and (absent --warn-only) exit non-zero -- directly falsifying the claim that every tracked file fits its budget. The active waiver enumerates exactly the 10 known violations; a NEW, unenumerated violation would still surface in the verifier's own warning output for the next waiver-renewal review to catch.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-04 (P89 89-02) — bans v0.13+ phase-ID tokens (regex \bP[0-9]{2,3}-[0-9]+\b) in production Rust source. Rust-native sibling of scripts/banned-words-lint.sh (docs-scoped); allowlist marker is `// banned-words: ok`. The regex intentionally misses v0.8/v0.9 audit IDs (P1-1 shape) — see the 89-02 script header for the trade-off. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P89 catalog-first commit (89-01); verifier ships in 89-02.

asserts: grep -nHE '\bP[0-9]{2,3}-[0-9]+\b' over crates/**/*.rs (excluding tests/, **/CHANGELOG.md, allowlist-marker lines) returns zero matches
violation lines emit owner_hint pointing at allowlist marker convention

rows.owner_hint: add '// banned-words: ok' allowlist marker OR rename the phase-id token
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts `grep -nHE '\bP[0-9]{2,3}-[0-9]+\b' crates/**/*.rs` (excluding tests/ and allowlist-marker lines and **/CHANGELOG.md) returns zero matches. If the description's claim ('phase IDs are banned in production source') were false — e.g., a `P79-02 scaffold` literal stayed in `crates/reposix-cli/src/attach.rs` — the assertion would fail because grep would report the match line and the verifier would exit 1.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-05 (P89 89-05) — cross-references deferral pointers in crates/ ('not yet wired in P\d+' / 'lands? (alongside|in) P\d+' / 'substrate-gap-deferred') against the named phase's PLAN file existence; ALSO BLOCKs explicitly on bare deferral matches carrying NO phase number. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P89 catalog-first commit (89-01); verifier ships in 89-05.

asserts: every match of 'not yet wired in P\d+' / 'lands? (alongside|in) P\d+' / 'substrate-gap-deferred' in crates/ resolves to .planning/phases/N-*/PLAN*.md existing
missing PLAN file BLOCKs with structured stderr naming the file:line + missing phase number
deferral pattern match with NO phase number (PNN suffix) BLOCKs explicitly rather than silently passing

rows.owner_hint: create the named phase's PLAN file or scrub the deferral pointer or add the missing PNN suffix
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts that for every match of three deferral patterns (`not yet wired in P\d+`, `lands? (alongside|in) P\d+`, `substrate-gap-deferred`) in `crates/`, the named phase number resolves to a `.planning/phases/N-*/` directory containing at least one `*PLAN*.md` file AND that any deferral-pattern match yields >=1 extracted phase number (else BLOCK explicitly with stderr citing the missing PNN suffix). If the description's claim ('deferral pointers cross-reference an existing downstream PLAN') were false — e.g., a comment said `// substrate-gap-deferred until P150 lands` and no .planning/phases/150-* exists, OR a comment said `// substrate-gap-deferred` with no phase number at all — the assertion would fail because the find command would return empty (orphan-PNN case) OR the PNN-extraction would yield zero matches (silent-pass case) and the verifier would exit 1 naming the bad reference.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-11 (P89 89-07) — load-time runner cross-check: every catalog row minted on/after 2026-05-08 must carry a claim_vs_assertion_audit string >=50 chars stating how the verifier's asserts would falsify the row's description claim if false; kind:shell-subprocess rows must ALSO declare a transcript-emitting contract (transcript_path). Pre-cutoff legacy rows (P78-P88) validate without the field until P95 RBF-D-06 backfills. Minted NOT-VERIFIED in the P89 catalog-first commit (89-01), which eats its own dogfood — all 6 P89 rows carry the field; verifier ships in 89-07.

asserts: load_catalog() refuses any catalog containing a row with last_verified >= 2026-05-08 OR last_verified=null AND missing claim_vs_assertion_audit string >=50 chars
load_catalog() additionally refuses any row with kind: shell-subprocess that lacks expected.artifact.transcript_path (or equivalent transcript-emitting contract)
SystemExit message names the catalog path + row id + cutoff date

rows.owner_hint: add a claim_vs_assertion_audit paragraph (>=50 chars) to the row explaining how the verifier would falsify the description claim if false; for kind:shell-subprocess rows ALSO declare a transcript path
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier asserts that `python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_audit_field` constructs a synthetic catalog containing a row dated 2026-05-08 with no `claim_vs_assertion_audit` field and that load_catalog() refuses it with `SystemExit(FAIL: ...missing claim_vs_assertion_audit (>=50 chars required for rows minted on/after 2026-05-08))`, AND additionally that any row declaring `kind: shell-subprocess` without an emitting-transcript contract triggers `SystemExit(FAIL: ...kind=shell-subprocess row missing transcript_path)`. If the description's claim ('the runner enforces the field on rows minted >= 2026-05-08, grandfathers older rows, AND enforces transcript_path on kind:shell-subprocess rows') were false — e.g., the date-cutoff was hard-coded to a future date, the field check was absent, or the kind=shell-subprocess sub-rule was missing — the assertion would fail because the synthetic catalogs would load successfully and the test would error.

rows.comment: Stage-2 catalog-row minting (2026-07-04). Prerequisite doc fixes (eager-window ledger items) landed in the same phase: docs/reference/cli.md gained attach + sync sections, docs/reference/exit-codes.md gained attach + sync rows plus re-derived (fn-name, not line-number) citations, docs/reference/crates.md fixed 'nine crates' -> ten + added a reposix-quality section.

asserts: every variant of the clap Cmd enum in crates/reposix-cli/src/main.rs (except Version, which has no dedicated cli.md prose section by long-standing convention) has a matching '## `reposix <name>`' section in docs/reference/cli.md
every variant (including Version) has a row in docs/reference/exit-codes.md's per-subcommand table

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a new/renamed Cmd enum variant in crates/reposix-cli/src/main.rs has no matching section in docs/reference/cli.md or no row in docs/reference/exit-codes.md -- add both in the same PR
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier regex-parses `enum Cmd { ... }` in main.rs for variant names, then for each non-exempt variant greps docs/reference/cli.md for a `## `reposix <name>`` heading (kebab-cased) and for every variant greps docs/reference/exit-codes.md for a backtick-wrapped row naming that subcommand. If the claim ('every CLI subcommand is documented in both reference pages') were false -- e.g. a new `reposix attach` variant shipped with no cli.md section -- the heading regex would find zero matches for 'attach' and the verifier would exit 1 naming the undocumented variant.

rows.comment: D-CONV-3 (2026-07-04, quality/SURPRISES.md 'Quality Convergence'): the scripts/ collapse deleted 6 dead shims + ~15 one-shot phase scripts, collapsed a duplicate validator pair, and converted quality/catalogs/orphan-scripts.json to the cadences-list schema. This row is the INVERSE gate that keeps the collapse from silently regressing -- the existing orphan-scripts-audit.py --all mode only re-checks rows that already exist, so it is structurally blind to a brand-new file dropped in scripts/ with no registry row.

asserts: every file directly under scripts/ (excluding the scripts/migrations/ subdirectory, exempt by policy) has EITHER a matching row in quality/catalogs/orphan-scripts.json OR is referenced by a live-wiring surface (.githooks/, .github/workflows/, docs/, CLAUDE.md, quality/gates/, quality/catalogs/, quality/PROTOCOL.md, or .claude/skills/)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a new file landed in scripts/ (excl. migrations/) with no orphan-scripts.json row and no reference from any live-wiring surface -- either add a registry row (mirroring an existing one) or wire it into a hook/workflow/doc and let this gate's grep pick it up.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If a new orphan script were added to scripts/ without a registry row or any live-wiring reference, the inverse scan's per-file loop would find it absent from both the `registered` id-set (derived from orphan-scripts.json rows) and every haystack file's text (grepped for `scripts/<basename>`), append it to `orphans`, and flip the row to FAIL naming the exact orphaned path -- the completeness claim is falsified the moment a single untracked file exists.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-06 (P90 90-02) — transport/perf-claim rows must carry coverage_kind: real-backend or an explicit WAIVED+until_date; hard-enforced at catalog-load time for rows minted >= P90 (D90-05), RAISE-only for legacy P78–P88 rows until P95 RBF-D-06 migrates them. transport_claim is a tri-state: explicit true/false wins, absent falls back to a regex over comment+id for dims perf/agent-ux/security (P90-D1).

asserts: is_transport_or_perf_row honors transport_claim tri-state (true→transport, false→suppress, absent→regex over comment+id in dims perf/agent-ux/security)
a transport-detected row minted >= P90 lacking coverage_kind AND lacking a compliant waiver → SystemExit at load
a legacy (pre-cutoff) transport row lacking coverage_kind loads clean (RAISE-only)
non-transport mechanical row without coverage_kind loads clean (no over-fire)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/_audit_field.py gains is_transport_or_perf_row() + the coverage_kind hard-check; verifier ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_audit_field.TestCoverageKind, which constructs synthetic rows exercising all three transport_claim states plus the minted_at cutoff split. If the description's claim ("a new transport/perf row minted at P90 or later without coverage_kind or a compliant waiver is rejected at load, while legacy pre-cutoff rows only RAISE") were false — e.g. the hard/RAISE split were inverted, or the transport_claim tri-state collapsed to a boolean and ignored the explicit-false override — the synthetic new-row case would load silently (no SystemExit) or the legacy-row case would wrongly hard-block, and the corresponding assertion would fail.

rows.comment: D90-03 (cross-AI H2 drain) — minted_at becomes the write-once audit-cutoff anchor for claim_vs_assertion_audit enforcement: when present it is the sole anchor (immutable), and the validator anchors on it in preference to the legacy last_verified heuristic. A row minted >= P90_MINT_CUTOFF (2026-07-05Z) without minted_at is rejected only via the existing last_verified>=cutoff guard; null-last_verified legacy rows are not retroactively forced (P90-D2 residual, drains at P95 RBF-D-06).

asserts: when minted_at present it is the sole audit-cutoff anchor (immutable; a hand-edited last_verified no longer moves the cutoff)
a row with minted_at >= P90_MINT_CUTOFF (2026-07-05Z) and no minted_at is rejected only via the lv>=cutoff guard (null-lv legacy rows NOT forced)
full-catalog load sweep succeeds at landing (no existing row trips the reject)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/_audit_field.py: anchor claim_vs_assertion_audit cutoff on minted_at when present; verifier ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs python3 -m unittest quality.runners.test_audit_field.TestMintedAt, which includes a backdate-attack fixture: a row carrying minted_at=2026-06-... alongside a hand-edited last_verified=2026-04-... (an attempt to dodge the cutoff by rewriting the older, no-longer-authoritative field). If the description's claim ("minted_at, once present, is the sole immutable audit-cutoff anchor") were false — e.g. the validator still consulted last_verified when minted_at was present — that backdate fixture would load without the claim_vs_assertion_audit requirement being enforced, and the test asserting rejection would fail. A second fixture asserts the full live catalog sweep still loads clean at landing time, guarding against an over-eager anchor swap breaking existing rows.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-07a (cross-AI H4 drain) — a row whose verifier.script path does not exist on disk at grade time must flip to NOT-VERIFIED, never silently preserve a stale PASS. The artifact distinguishes a deploy/path glitch (error: verifier not found at <path>) from a real regression FAIL so on-call triage doesn't confuse the two. The transient _verifier_missing marker is stripped before the artifact is persisted.

asserts: a PASS row whose verifier.script is absent flips to NOT-VERIFIED (never preserves PASS)
the artifact carries error='verifier not found at <path>' distinguishing a deploy glitch from a real FAIL
_verifier_missing transient flag is stripped before persist

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py: missing-verifier-script branch demotes to NOT-VERIFIED with the error marker; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs a new test class asserting that a synthetic catalog row with status: PASS and a verifier.script path pointed at a nonexistent file, when re-graded, ends up with status NOT-VERIFIED and an artifact containing error='verifier not found at <path>', with no residual _verifier_missing key persisted. If the description's claim ("a missing verifier script demotes PASS to NOT-VERIFIED rather than preserving the stale grade") were false — e.g. today's single preserve-everything branch stayed intact — the fixture's re-grade would leave status: PASS untouched and the assertion would fail.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-07b (cross-AI M8, AMENDED D90-04 per D90-12 item 2) — env-gated skip of pre-release-real-backend rows (creds/allowlist missing) is fail-closed WITH preserved history: current status flips and PERSISTS NOT-VERIFIED for ALL such rows including the P0 litmus (never a skip-as-pass channel, OD-2-safe), while the prior REAL grade survives in last_real_grade + last_real_verified write-history fields (written only when the prior status was PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL), and the artifact/report carries skip_reason: env-missing so the churn is explained. A second cred-less run is idempotent (byte-identical catalog).

asserts: an env-gated skip flips and persists status NOT-VERIFIED for ALL pre-release-real-backend rows including the P0 litmus (fail-closed, OD-2-safe)
the prior real grade is preserved in last_real_grade + last_real_verified write-history fields (written only when prior status was PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL)
the skip artifact/report carries skip_reason: env-missing so the churn is explained
a second cred-less run after the flip leaves the catalog byte-identical (idempotent)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/_realbackend.py: env-gate skip branch gains fail-closed-with-history semantics; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: MUST match the fail-closed-with-history contract (NOT the rejected preserve-PASS wording). The verifier runs quality.runners.test_realbackend.TestRunRowEnvGateIntegration, which fixtures a row previously graded PASS by a real run, then re-runs it with REPOSIX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS and creds scrubbed from the environment. If the description's claim ("a cred-less run can never ride a stale real PASS; history + skip_reason explain the demotion") were false, the test would observe one of two failure shapes: status still PASS (the skip-as-pass channel OD-2 forbids reopening), or status NOT-VERIFIED with no last_real_grade/skip_reason recorded (silent ground-truth loss — M8's actual complaint). Either observation fails the assertion; only the fail-closed-with-history shape passes.

rows.comment: RBF-FW-08 (cross-AI M6) — kind:shell-subprocess rows declare a transcript-emitting contract at load time (RBF-FW-11 precedent), but nothing today enforces the contract was actually FULFILLED at grade time. This row closes that gap: a kind:shell-subprocess row that computed PASS on verifier exit 0 is downgraded to FAIL at grade time unless artifact.transcript_path names a file that actually exists and contains an argv: line, proving a real subprocess transcript was written, not merely promised in the catalog row.

asserts: a kind:shell-subprocess row computing PASS on exit 0 is downgraded to FAIL unless artifact.transcript_path names an existing file containing an argv: line
non-shell-subprocess rows are unaffected (no over-fire)
the live agent-ux/kind-shell-subprocess-worked-example row still PASSes (its verifier emits a real transcript)

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py: _run_synthetic gains a post-exit-0 transcript-existence check for kind:shell-subprocess rows; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier extends quality.runners.test_realbackend._run_synthetic with a kind:shell-subprocess fixture whose verifier script exits 0 but never writes the transcript file it names. If the description's claim ("a shell-subprocess row cannot PASS on exit-0 alone; the transcript must actually exist with an argv: line") were false — e.g. the runner trusted exit code alone, as it does today — that fixture would grade PASS despite the missing transcript, and the assertion would fail. A companion fixture asserts non-shell-subprocess rows are unaffected, and a live-catalog fixture asserts the real agent-ux/kind-shell-subprocess-worked-example row still PASSes because its verifier genuinely emits a transcript.

rows.comment: F-K4b / ROADMAP SC2 (confirmed shipping per D90-12 item 1: per-expected-assert design ruled by D90-12 item 1, defer-to-P95 rejected) — today's grading only checks that verifier-written asserts_passed and a row's expected.asserts share SOME vocabulary overlap globally, which let the p86 F6 shape (9 expected vs 17 passed, heavy shared vocabulary, 2 expected uncovered) grade PASS dishonestly. This row requires per-expected-assert coverage: when both lists are non-empty, EVERY expected.asserts entry must map to at least one asserts_passed entry via normalized per-pair token matching; any unmatched expected assert blocks the PASS flip and asserts_failed names the unmatched entries. The check is a no-op when asserts_passed is empty (backward-compat with the ~91 pre-existing mechanical rows that don't yet emit per-assert results).

asserts: when BOTH expected.asserts and verifier-written asserts_passed are non-empty, EVERY expected.asserts entry must map to >=1 asserts_passed entry via normalized per-pair token matching; any unmatched expected assert blocks the PASS flip and asserts_failed names the unmatched entries
when asserts_passed is empty (verifier wrote none) the check is a no-op (backward-compat with the 91 mechanical rows)
the p86 F6 shape (9 expected vs 17 passed, heavy shared vocabulary, 2 expected uncovered) goes RED
zero false RED across all existing catalog rows against their current artifacts

rows.owner_hint: quality/runners/run.py: grade-time per-expected-assert congruence check; ships 90-02
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The verifier runs quality.runners.test_run.TestAssertsCongruence, whose central fixture reproduces the p86 F6 shape verbatim (9 expected.asserts vs 17 asserts_passed, heavy shared vocabulary, 2 of the 9 expected entries genuinely uncovered by any passed entry). If the description's claim ("every expected assert must be covered by at least one passed assert via per-pair matching, not merely global vocabulary overlap") were false — e.g. only a global bag-of-tokens overlap check ran, as it does today — the F6 fixture would grade PASS (the shared vocabulary masks the 2 uncovered entries) and the assertion expecting RED would fail. A second fixture sweeps every existing catalog row against its live artifact and asserts zero false RED, guarding against an over-eager per-pair matcher breaking honest existing PASSes.
